title: HIV and AIDS (Prevention and Control) Regulations, 2020
title_en: HIV and AIDS (Prevention and Control) Regulations, 2020

provisions.title: Citation
provisions.content: These Regulations may be cited as the HIV and AIDS (Prevention and Control) Regulations, 2020.

provisions.title: Interpretation
provisions.content: In these Regulations, unless the context otherwise requires— “ Act ” means the HIV and AIDS (Prevention and Control) Act; [Cap. 431] “ AIDS ” has the meaning ascribed to it under the Act ; “ blood products ” means any product derived from human blood, and includes blood plasma, platelets, red or white blood corpuscles and other products derived from licensed products; “ body fluids ” means blood and other body fluids from human body that may be infectious and include semen, vaginal secretions and amniotic fluid; “ child ” has the meaning ascribed to it under the Act ; “ discrimination ” means a distinction, exclusion or restriction, whether direct or indirect, made on the basis of the actual or perceived HIV status of a person living with or affected by HIV which has the effect or purpose of impairing or nullifying the recognition, enjoyment or exercise by that person on the basis of equality with other members of the community, on the human rights and fundamental freedoms in the political, economic, social, cultural, civil or any other field; “ disposal ” means the discharge, deposit, injection, dumping, spilling, leaking, abandoning or placing of medical waste in, on, into or onto land, other surface, building, vehicle, trailer or other containment structure or into any water, watercourse, storm water system or sewer system; “ health practitioner ” has the meaning ascribed to it under the Act ; “ HIV related commodities ” include HIV test devices and other laboratory reagents, medicines for treating sexually transmitted infections and opportunistic infections, condoms, contraceptives, micronutrients and consumable laboratory and medical supplies; “ HIV self-testing ” has the meaning ascribed to it under the Act ; “ HIV test kit ” means a package system of key components of analytical methods used to determine the presence of HIV antibodies; “ Human Immunodeficiency Virus (HIV) ” has the meaning ascribed to it under the Act ; “ medical practitioner ” has the meaning ascribed to it under the Medical, Dental and Allied Health Professionals Act ; [Cap. 152] “ medical waste ” includes infectious waste, pathological wastes, sharps , pharmaceuticals, genetoxics, radioactive wastes, coagulated blood wastes and drugs and any other waste generated as a result of health care provision at home, health care facilities, research facilities or laboratories; “ Minister ” means the Minister responsible for matters relating to health; “ Ministry ” means the Ministry responsible for matters relating to health; “ NACP ” has the meaning ascribed to it under the Act ; “ National HIV and AIDS Research Fellowship Committee ” means the Committee established under section 36 of the Act ; “ Person Living With HIV (PLHIV) ” means a person whose HIV test results reveal the presence of HIV or HIV antibodies; “ Post Exposure Prophylaxis ” means the immediate provision of preventive measures and medication following an exposure to potentially infected blood or other body fluids in order to minimise the risk of acquiring HIV infection; “ sharps ” includes anything capable of puncturing or cutting skin such as scissor, needle, scalpel or blade; “ stigma ” has the meaning ascribed to it under the Act ; “ TACAIDS ” means the Tanzania Commission for AIDS established under the Tanzania Commission for AIDS Act . [Cap. 379] Part II – Pre-test counselling

provisions.title: Provision of pretest counselling
provisions.content: (1) The Ministry shall provide user friendly information and education materials on HIV information that is responsive to the needs of people. (2) Prior to performing an HIV test, a client shall be provided pre-test counselling. (3) For the purpose of allowing client-specific counselling, a counsellor may tailor HIV counselling to meet the best needs of the person to be tested. (4) The tailoring and pre-test counselling shall be made on a case-to-case basis and a client may be tested for HIV and shall entail— (a) creating rapport and checking client’s understanding on HIV; (b) description of the interventions that reduce HIV transmission; (c) meaning of positive, negative and inconclusive test results; (d) assurance of confidentiality; (e) opportunity to ask questions; (f) voluntariness of the test and the need for the client’s consent; (g) requirement of signing consent form for clients of the age of below fifteen years; and (h) assurance that the client will not be denied any other health service for declining to be tested. Part III – Requirement for offering HIV testing and counselling

provisions.title: Testing and counselling
provisions.content: (1) A person who intends to undergo HIV testing shall be counseled and tested in accordance with the provisions of these Regulations. (2) Without prejudice to subregulation 1 , testing and counselling shall be done in accordance with the guidelines for HIV testing and counselling issued by the Ministry .

provisions.title: Consent for HIV testing and counselling
provisions.content: (1) A person who intends to undergo HIV testing and counselling shall give consent to undergo HIV testing verbally or in writing by filling the form set out in the First Schedule. (2) The offering of HIV testing and counselling by a health practitioner shall be required in clinical setup so as to promote earlier detection of HIV with unrecognised risk factors, including— (a) services or treatment for sexually transmitted infections and tuberculosis; (b) clinical services for drug users unless such test is deemed inappropriate by a medical practitioner or health practitioner caring for the client; and (c) any other outpatients or inpatients. (3) For avoidance of doubt, HIV testing shall be included in the routine package of prenatal, labour and delivery for all pregnant women, lactating mothers and men responsible for the pregnancy, unless the testing is declined. (4) For the purpose of this regulation, “clinical setup” means a setting or place for the provision of health care services at a health facility or at community setting.

provisions.title: Exceptions to consent requirement
provisions.content: (1) Notwithstanding regulation 5 , medical practitioner or health practitioner may secure a sample for HIV testing without consent under the following circumstances: (a) in case of a child or a person with inability to comprehend the results, a written consent of a parent, recognised guardian or legal representative shall be required; (b) on the donor of human organs and tissues; (c) where it is required by the court order; and (d) for sexual offenders: Provided that, reasonable efforts shall be made to secure voluntary consent. (2) Without prejudice to subregulation 1 , a medical practitioner or health practitioner responsible for the treatment of a person living with HIV, may undertake HIV testing in respect of that person without the consent of the person where— (a) the person is unconscious and unable to give consent; and (b) the medical practitioner reasonably believes that such a test is clinically necessary or desirable for the interest of that person. (3) Consent of a parent, recognised guardian or legal representative required under subregulation (1)(a) shall be in the form set out in the Second Schedule.

provisions.title: Qualification of HIV testing service provider
provisions.content: (1) A person shall not conduct HIV testing and counselling unless such person has successfully undergone training in accordance with the national HIV testing and counselling curriculum approved by the Ministry . (2) In addition to training referred to under subregulation (1) , a person who intends to conduct HIV testing shall be licensed under the Health Laboratory Practitioners Act. [Cap.48]

provisions.title: Requirement for performing testing
provisions.content: (1) A person shall not provide HIV test services without first providing appropriate information and counselling necessary to facilitate the acquisition of consent from the client. (2) Subject to the provisions of subregulation (1) , the consent and counselling shall be documented in the client's medical record. Part IV – HIV testing

provisions.title: Requirement for HIV testing centres
provisions.content: The owner, manager or person in-charge of a testing centre shall ensure the availability of HIV testing equipment, supplies and qualified personnel as approved by the relevant authority.

provisions.title: HIV testing algorithm
provisions.content: (1) HIV testing shall be done in accordance with National HIV testing algorithm and guidelines for HIV testing and counselling issued by the Ministry . (2) Subject to subregulation 1 , the National HIV testing algorithm and guidelines shall be emphasized to every person who undertakes HIV testing in accordance with applicable national standards.

provisions.title: HIV testing quality assurance
provisions.content: For the purpose of ensuring quality HIV testing, a HIV testing services provider, medical or health practitioner shall comply with guidelines for quality improvement of HIV and AIDS services issued by the Ministry and the National Laboratory Quality Assurance Framework issued by the Health Laboratory Practitioners’ Council. Part V – HIV self-testing , distribution, handling and disposal of self-test kits

provisions.title: Self-testing
provisions.content: (1) Without prejudice to other provisions of these Regulations, a person of the age of eighteen years or above may undertake HIV self-testing in the manner provided under these Regulation: Provided that, HIV self-testing is for screening purposes, therefore an individual shall be required to visit a health facility or authorised HIV testing services for confirmation of results. (2) HIV self-testing may be performed in the following ways: (a) directly assisted, whereby an individual receives a direct in-person demonstration by a trained healthcare provider or peer with instructions on how to perform a self-test and how to interpret the self-test results; or (b) unassisted, whereby an individual performs a HIV test independently following the instructions in the insert or leaflet of the self-test kit. (3) HIV self-testing shall be voluntary and without any form of coercion. (4) For the purposes of this regulation, HIV selftesting is not intended to replace but rather complement the existing HIV testing modalities by enabling more people to know their HIV status, especially those who are not reached by existing services.

provisions.title: HIV self-test kits
provisions.content: (1) HIV self-testing shall be performed using self-test kits which meet the minimum standards set by the relevant authorities. (2) All HIV self-test kits shall include in the package an insert or leaflet containing instructions for use and disposal written in clear and simple language. (3) A person may obtain self-test kits from— (a) a trained healthcare provider; (b) a registered seller; or (c) any other person authorised by the Ministry .

provisions.title: Quality assurance of self-test kits
provisions.content: (1) All self-test kits for national procurement shall attain WHO pre-qualification. (2) HIV self-test kits shall not be distributed for public use unless they have undergone in-country validation by the National health reference laboratory and any other relevant authority.

provisions.title: Distribution, sale, dispensing or supply of self-test kits
provisions.content: (1) HIV self-test kits may be available at or distributed through— (i) health care facilities; (ii) registered seller identified by the Ministry ; (iii) any other means as may be directed by the Ministry . (2) A person shall not distribute, sell, dispense or supply HIV self-test kits unless that person is trained and registered to provide HIV self-testing services. (3) The Ministry shall develop and maintain a database for all persons trained and registered to provide HIV self-testing services in a Government or private health facility.

provisions.title: Responsibility of provider of self-test kits
provisions.content: A provider of HIV self-test kits shall before attending a client— (a) satisfy himself that the client is of the age of eighteen years or above; (b) instruct the client on how to use the self-test kit; (c) advise the client to invite his or her sexual partner for testing; (d) provide pre-testing counselling to the client in accordance with the Ministry ’s guidelines; (e) inform the client that HIV self-test kit does not provide definite and conclusive diagnosis; (f) advise the client to visit a health facility or authorised HIV testing services for confirmation of results and other intergrated services stipulated in the relevant National HIV testing guidelines; and (g) instruct the client on the handling and disposal of used self-test kits as per guidelines.

provisions.title: Responsibility of user of self-test kits
provisions.content: A person who uses a HIV self-test kit shall ensure that the kit is obtained from an authorised distribution point and comply with the instructions contained in the manufacturer’s insert or leaflet and instructions given by the self-test kit provider including— (a) using a self-test kit once per each test; (b) handling a self-test kit in a proper manner; (c) disposing the used self-test kit in a proper and safe manner; (d) after obtaining self-test results, visiting a healthcare provider or facility for a confirmatory test.

provisions.title: Disposal of HIV self-test kits
provisions.content: (1) Used HIV self-test kits may be easily disposed-off in a bin or pit latrine as non- medical waste . (2) A person shall ensure that used self-test kits are properly and safely disposed as per instructions contained in the insert or leaflet or returned to the designated collection point or at a health facility. Part VI – Post - HIV test counselling

provisions.title: Individuals with negative HIV test results
provisions.content: (1) A person with negative HIV test results shall be provided with post test counselling by the HIV testing services provider. (2) The HIV testing services provider shall— (a) inform the client of the test results, its implications and the importance of retesting; (b) counsel the client on the importance of continuing to take protective measures to avoid acquiring the infection; (c) inform the client on the available HIV preventive services; (d) encourage the client on the importance of notifying his spouse or sexual partner to undergo HIV testing; and (e) in the case of a person who injects drugs, refer such person to a rehabilitation center or mental health clinic for earliest possible evaluation and treatment.

provisions.title: Individuals with positive HIV test results
provisions.content: (1) A person with positive HIV test results shall be provided with post-test counselling by the HIV testing services provider. (2) The counselling shall include information regarding to— (a) the test results and its implication; (b) the infectious nature of the disease and measures to prevent transmission and re-infection; (c) referral to care, treatment and support services; (d) for persons who inject drugs, referral to rehabilitation centers or mental health clinic for earliest possible evaluation and treatment; (e) the legal obligation of notifying and protecting others, including a spouse or sexual partner; (f) the legal consequences of willful infecting another person by not disclosing his status; (g) any other relevant information. (3) In addition to the information provided under subregulation 2 , where a pregnant woman tests positive for HIV, a medical practitioner or health practitioner shall provide post-test counselling, which shall include information about— (a) interventions to prevent the transmission of HIV from mother to child during pregnancy, labour, delivery and breast feeding including infant feeding, counselling and support; (b) the availability of, and referrals for, appropriate health care services, including mental health, nutritional and appropriate social and support services; (c) prevention of HIV transmission to others; and (d) HIV testing for spouse, sexual partner or family members.

provisions.title: Laboratory investigation request form
provisions.content: A medical or health practitioner who directs a HIV test to be undertaken shall complete the prescribed form as set out in the Third Schedule to these Regulations. Part VII – Records and confidentiality

provisions.title: Client’s records and confidentiality
provisions.content: (1) A health or medical practitioner shall put an entry of client’s records in the HIV testing services recording and reporting system for all services rendered. (2) The entries shall be contained in a report in the form set out in the Fourth Schedule to these Regulations. (3) All forms and reports under these Regulations shall be maintained in the HIV testing services recording and reporting system, including copies of any referral forms or reports submitted by one health facility to another as part of the plan of care and shall be done in accordance with the requirements of these Regulations. (4) All health practitioners, workers, employers, recruitment agencies, insurance companies, data recorders, sign language interpreters, legal guardians and other custodians of any medical records, files, data or test results shall be responsible for maintaining confidentiality of the data as provided for under the Act and shall take appropriate measures for their protection, including— (a) keeping records secure at all times and establishing adequate confidentiality to safeguard such records stored in any form whatsoever; (b) establishing and enforcing reasonable operational guidelines and procedures consistent with the confidentiality requirements; and (c) training individuals who handle records in security objectives and techniques.

provisions.title: Confidentiality and disclosure of HIV test results
provisions.content: (1) All information and reports pertaining to HIV counselling sessions, testing and reporting as set forth in these Regulations shall be confidential. (2) A person shall not disclose to a third party the results of an individual's HIV test without the prior written consent of that individual or in the case of a minor, the minor's parent, guardian or legal representative on a form that specifically states that HIV tests results may be released, except where— (a) an approved and registered health care facility or laboratory— (i) reports HIV test results to a medical practitioner ; or (ii) reports HIV test results to the Ministry in the form set out in Fourth Schedule of these Regulations; (b) a medical practitioner — (i) enters HIV test results in the medical record, as would be the case with any other diagnostic test; (ii) notifies other medical practitioners or health practitioners directly involved in care of the individual testing positive on the HIV test or to whom that individual is referred for treatment, using referral form set out in the Fifth Schedule to these Regulations; (iii) notifies persons exposed to blood or other body fluids of an individual who tests positive on an HIV test; (iv) notifies the parents, guardian or legal representative pursuant to these Regulations; and (v) informs third parties with whom an HIV infected client is in continuous exposure-related contact, including a spouse or sexual partner. (3) The provisions of subparagraphs (iii) , (iv) and (v) of subregulation (2)(b) shall apply where— (a) in the medical practitioner ’s opinion, the nature of the contact or exposure poses a clear and present danger of HIV transmission to the third party; and (b) the medical practitioner has reason to believe that the client, despite the medical practitioner 's strong encouragement, has not and will not inform the third party that they may have been exposed to HIV. (4) Health facilities and health service providers shall, in writing, require every person with access to any confidential information to understand and acknowledge that the information shall not be disclosed except as provided for under these Regulations.

provisions.title: Reporting of HIV testing
provisions.content: (1) The owner, manager or person in charge of a testing center or laboratory, District Medical Officer and Regional Medical Officer shall report monthly to the Ministry on HIV testing services offered during the particular month, in a manner set out in the Fourth Schedule to these Regulations. (2) The reports provided for under this regulation shall be submitted through the official Ministry Report Forms. (3) Any person responsible for the administration of a clinical or hospital laboratory, blood bank, mobile unit or other facilities in which a laboratory examination of any specimen derived from a human body which yields serological, or other evidence of HIV, including perinatal exposure to HIV shall report to the Ministry in a prescribed manner. (4) Report required under this regulation shall be obtained from— (a) all health facilities obtaining blood from human donors for the purpose of transfusion or preparation of blood and blood products ; (b) all testing centers; and (c) any laboratory that processes specimens.

provisions.title: Inspection of health facilities or laboratory
provisions.content: The Ministry may inspect and examine the quality and records of the health facilities or laboratories in order to evaluate compliance with these Regulations. Part VIII – Logistics and use of ARVs and other HIV related commodities

provisions.title: Procurement, storage, distribution and of ARVs or HIV related commodities
provisions.content: (1) All transactions relating to procurement, distribution and storage of ARVs or HIV related commodities shall be governed by the relevant laws. (2) All donations of ARVs or other HIV related commodities shall comply with the Ministry ’s donation guidelines.

provisions.title: Maintaining quality of ARVs or other HIV related commodities through supply chain
provisions.content: (1) A person who procures, stores or distributes ARVs or other HIV related commodities shall maintain the quality, safety and efficacy within the supply chain. (2) Subject to the provisions of subregulation (1) , the supply chain shall be done by the Government procurement agency or recognised agent.

provisions.title: Ensuring continuous supply of ARVs or HIV related commodities
provisions.content: (1) Every manager, owner or person in charge of a health facility shall ensure availability, proper report and management of ARVs or other HIV related commodities at the acceptable standards without interruption in order to avoid risks of unreliable supply of ARVs or HIV related commodities . (2) Reports and requests for ARVs or HIV related commodities shall be based on reliable estimates of actual need so as to avoid stock-outs or overstocks. (3) In organising the supply of ARVs or HIV related commodities , the Ministry may develop a public private supply plan. (4) The private sector may be involved in the financing and supply of ARVs or HIV related commodities .

provisions.title: Dispensing of ARVs or HIV related commodities
provisions.content: (1) Dispensing of ARVs or HIV related commodities shall be in line with the guidelines by the Ministry for the management of HIV and AIDS and other relevant laws. (2) The essential measures to ensure good dispensing of ARVs or HIV related commodities shall include— (a) training of pharmaceutical personnel and health practitioners in dispensing, use and side effects of ARVs or HIV related commodities according to the guidelines for the management of HIV and AIDS ; (b) training of pharmaceutical personnel and other health practitioners in communication skills; (c) provision of clear and simple instructions to clients on use of ARVs or HIV related commodities and their adverse effects; and (d) establishment of a relationship based on confidence between the client and pharmaceutical personnel or other health practitioners.

provisions.title: Drugs supplied for free by ministry
provisions.content: (1) The ARVs or HIV related commodities shall, where supplied for free by the Ministry to private health facilities, not be sold to the clients. (2) The Ministry may allow ARVs or HIV related commodities to be procured and sold by the private facilities. (3) Sale of ARVs or HIV related commodities in private facilities shall be done with prescription from a medical practitioner .

provisions.title: Follow-up on adverse reaction and events of ARVs and HIV related commodities
provisions.content: (1) A medical practitioner or health practitioner shall make a follow up to individual clients for adverse ARVs or HIV related commodities reactions and toxicity. (2) A medical practitioner or a health practitioner shall recognise and record the adverse reaction and events associated with ARVs or HIV related commodities and report to the relevant authority. (3) Subject to subregulations (1) and (2) , a medical practitioner or health practitioner shall ensure that ARVs or HIV related commodities with adverse reaction and events for each client are recorded in an individual’s file and reported using the form set out in the Sixth Schedule to these Regulations. Part IX – Precautions to prevent transmission of HIV

provisions.title: Medical and health practitioner to take precaution
provisions.content: (1) A medical practitioner or health practitioner shall take due precaution by wearing appropriate personal protective equipment when undertaking clinical procedure for the purpose of protecting himself and the patient from infection. (2) Managers, owners or persons in-charge of health facilities shall ensure availability and accessibility of appropriate protective gears and infection control supplies in their facilities. (3) A medical practitioner or health practitioner shall routinely use appropriate safety precautions to prevent skin and mucous membrane into contact with blood and other body fluids of the client in accordance with the Infection Prevention and Control Guidelines.

provisions.title: Safe injection
provisions.content: A medical practitioner or health practitioner shall adhere to safe injection practices which shall include— (a) single use of needle and syringe; (b) skin preparation for injection; and (c) disposal of needle and syringe in a puncture resistant container placed within arm’s reach at the point of use.

provisions.title: Invasive procedure
provisions.content: (1) A medical or health practitioner who performs or participates in an invasive procedure or performs a function ancillary to an invasive procedure shall, in the performance of or participation in any such procedure or function be familiar with, observe and rigorously adhere to both general infection control practices in universal blood and body fluid precautions to minimise the risk of HIV transmission from medical or health practitioner to a client or from a client to a medical or health practitioner as the case may be. (2) For the purpose of this regulation, “invasive procedure” means a medical procedure that invades or enters the body usually by cutting or puncturing the skin or by inserting instruments into the body.

provisions.title: Duty to take precautions by medical practitioner or practitioner
provisions.content: (1) A medical practitioner or a health practitioner and supporting staff shall take appropriate precautions to prevent injuries caused by sharp instruments and other devices— (a) procedures; (b) disinfecting, cleaning and sterilisation of used instruments; (c) management and disposal of used sharp instruments and healthcare wastes; and (d) handling of used sharp instruments and health facility linen. (2) Subject to provisions of subregulation (1) , procedure for management and disposal of used sharp instruments, other devices and healthcare wastes shall be in accordance with the standards and guidelines issued by the Ministry. (3) The manager, owner or person in-charge of a health facility shall ensure availability and access of waste storage and disposal facilities as stipulated in the standards and guidelines issued by the Ministry .

provisions.title: HIV campaigns or outreach services
provisions.content: (1) Any person who intends to conduct HIV campaign or HIV outreach services shall inform the relevant authority in writing and the authority may appoint a representative to attend the campaign or outreach services. (2) Notwithstanding subregulation (1) , HIV campaigns or outreach services conducted in schools shall— (a) require written approval from the Ministry responsible for education and the Ministry responsible for local government authorities; and (b) not involve HIV testing. (2) A person who conducts HIV campaign or HIV outreach services shall at all times ensure proper management of medical waste in accordance with the Ministry ’s standards and guidelines. (3) Medical waste shall be properly managed by the person undertaking HIV campaign or outreach services and disposed off in a nearby health facility in compliance with the guidelines: Provided that, in conducting such campaign or outreach services as the case may be, the District Medical Officer shall be involved and his role shall be to supervise the proper management of such activities.

provisions.title: Precautions during emergencies
provisions.content: (1) The owner, manager or person in-charge of a health facility or medical or health practitioner who comes in contact with blood or body fluids shall take all necessary precautions to prevent himself and the person at the emergency situation from the risk of being infected with HIV. (2) Any person other than a medical or health practitioner who comes into contact with the blood or body fluids shall report the matter to the owner, manager or person in-charge of a health facility or medical or health practitioner immediately.

provisions.title: Resuscitation devices
provisions.content: For the purposes of minimizing the need for emergency mouth to mouth resuscitation and other resuscitation, the manager, owner, person in-charge of a health facility shall ensure that mouthpieces, resuscitation bags or other ventilation devices are available for use in areas in which the need for resuscitation is predictable.

provisions.title: Sterilization and disinfection
provisions.content: The owner, manager or person in charge of a health facility or health practitioner who uses instruments or devices which enter into the tissue or the vascular system of any client or through which blood flows shall ensure that such instruments or devices undergo sterilization or receive high-level disinfection before reuse. Part X – Parenteral exposure

provisions.title: Sharps disposal
provisions.content: The owner, manager or person in-charge of a health facility or medical or health practitioner shall ensure that all sharps are safely kept to prevent potential harm and transmission of HIV by— (a) disposing sharps in puncture resistant containers; (b) disposing sharps directly, without manipulation such as recap, remove or bend needles; (c) disposing sharps immediately after use at the point of generation; and (d) incinerating as per national infection, prevention and control standards.

provisions.title: Disposal of body parts
provisions.content: All health care facilities shall ensure proper disposal of all body parts, body fluids and blood that have been contaminated with HIV as follows: (a) body parts shall be incinerated; (b) placentas, blood, body fluids or specimen shall be incinerated or disposed in the placentas pits; and (c) wet dressing, catheters or blood infusion bags shall be incinerated.

provisions.title: Handling of sharps container
provisions.content: A health practitioner who handles sharps container shall ensure that— (a) sharps container is placed in a visible position; (b) sharps container is marked clearly to make it easily identifiable and distinguished from other containers used for disposing of other types of medical waste ; (c) sharps container is not shaken to settle its contents and make room for more sharps ; (d) sharps container is not placed in high traffic areas, outside patient rooms or procedure rooms; (e) sharps container is made up of puncture-resistant material; (f) sharps container is a disposable one; (g) any sharp placed in the container is not manipulated inside the container and shall not be removed from the said container under any circumstances; (h) sharps are placed directly in the container without recapping, clipping, bending or breaking, unless it is proved that— (i) such recapping or needle removal is accomplished through the use of mechanical device; or (ii) the requirements of this regulation are not feasible for a specific medical procedure; and (i) sharps container is disposed as a whole unit by incinerating.

provisions.title: Requirements for internal transport of medical waste
provisions.content: (1) The owner, manager or person in charge of a health facility who transports medical waste shall ensure that he undertakes precaution steps so as to avoid risk or harm to any person. (2) Internal transportation of medical waste shall be conducted in accordance with the national infection, prevention and control standards and other relevant laws. (3) Without prejudice to subregulation (2) , a transporter of medical waste shall— (a) take reasonable measures to ensure that, once medical waste is placed in a container, it is not removed from that container in order to decant it into another container or sort it until such waste is disposed; (b) by using available resources, minimise the manual handling of sharp containers during internal transportation to prevent injuries to any person; and (c) transport the waste by means of wheeled containers, trolleys or carts that are not used for any other purposes. (4) Internal transportation of medical waste shall be done by trained personnel or person assigned and not otherwise. Part XI – Post exposure prophylaxis

provisions.title: Provision for post exposure prophylaxis
provisions.content: (1) A medical or health practitioner shall ensure that a person who has been accidentally exposed to blood or body fluids , or in any other way, is offered post exposure prophylaxis for the management of that exposure in accordance with the guidelines issued by the Ministry . (2) A health practitioner in-charge shall ensure better provision of post exposure prophylaxis services and that such services are available for twenty-four hours on each day, including weekends.

provisions.title: Management of exposure site
provisions.content: (1) A medical or health practitioner shall ensure that wounds and skin sites that have been in contact with blood or body fluids from an exposed person are washed with clean water and mucous membrane is flushed with water. (2) Upon the occurrence of occupational exposure under subregulation (1) , a medical or health practitioner shall— (a) ensure that the exposure site is treated as soon as practicable; (b) evaluate the exposure and its source; (c) document and report the exposure to the relevant authority; (d) counsel and test for HIV; (e) provide or assist in the provision of post exposure prophylaxis; and (f) make a follow-up of a person exposed to HIV.

provisions.title: Risk assessment for occupational exposure
provisions.content: In determining risk of exposure, a medical or health practitioner shall use either of the following criteria: (a) percutaneous injury; (b) mucus membrane exposure; (c) non intact skin exposure; or (d) bites resulting to blood exposure to either person involved.

provisions.title: Procedure for post exposure prophylaxis
provisions.content: (1) Any person who, in the performance of his duty or undertaking is exposed to blood or other body fluids of another person, shall document a complaint in accordance with the guidelines for the management of HIV and AIDS or guidelines for HIV and AIDS workplace intervention issued by the Ministry , provided— (a) there has been a significant percutaneous or mucous membrane exposure through needle stick, bite, splash over open wound, broken skin or such membrane by blood or body fluids of the person and such exposure is likely to lead to transmission of HIV; (b) he completes an incident report within forty eight hours of the exposure, identifying the parties to the exposure, witnesses, time, place and nature of the event; and (c) he submits to a baseline HIV test within seventy two hours of the exposure and is negative on that test for the presence of HIV. (2) Where in the event that a licensed health care facility, a medical practitioner , health practitioner or an occupational health representative in a supervisory position to the person making the determination had an exposure to the blood or body fluids of a client and the client's guardian refuses to grant consent for an HIV test to determine whether the client is infected with HIV, and— (a) if a sample of the client's blood is available, the said blood shall be tested for the HIV; or (b) if a sample of the client's blood is not otherwise available and the client refuses to grant consent, the medical practitioner or health practitioner may sign acknowledging the refusal. (3) In an emergency, where due to a grave medical or psychiatric condition, it is impossible to obtain consent from either the client or the client's parent, recognised guardian or legal representative, the medical practitioner or health practitioner may proceed with the HIV test without such consent. Part XII – Conducting of HIV vaccines and other related trials

provisions.title: Requirement for application to conduct HIV vaccine or related trial
provisions.content: A person who intends to conduct HIV vaccine or other related trial shall make an application to the relevant authority.

provisions.title: Obligation to provide education
provisions.content: A person who intends to conduct HIV vaccines or other related trials shall educate the intended population on the effects (if any) and advantages of the intended vaccines or investigational products as the case may be.

provisions.title: Research project and findings
provisions.content: (1) Any person who intends to conduct a research project on HIV and AIDS shall require approval of the National HIV and AIDS Research Fellowship Committee . (2) A person granted approval by the Committee shall— (a) comply with any other written laws relating to research including obtaining ethical clearance from relevant bodies; (b) prior to conducting the research, submit to TACAIDS and NACP a research proposal and any other information as may be required; (c) upon completion of the research, submit a report to TACAIDS and NACP for compilation of findings and submission to the Minister.

provisions.title: Applicability and availability of research findings
provisions.content: TACAIDS and NACP shall ensure that the disseminated research findings are at all times applicable and available to the public. Part XIII – Prohibition of stigma and discrimination

provisions.title: Prohibition of use of discriminatory words
provisions.content: (1) A person shall not use discriminatory words or acts to a person living with HIV, orphan or their families. (2) A statement shall be deemed to be discriminatory if its contents exclusively express some biasness, inequitableness, one-sidedness, favoritism, prejudice, injustice, influence or any other thing or tendency which is likely to be regarded as discriminatory.

provisions.title: Prohibition of discriminatory publication
provisions.content: A person shall not display or cause to be displayed before the public any notice, sign, symbol, emblem or other representation that— (a) expresses or implies discrimination or an intention to discriminate; and (b) incites others to discriminate.

provisions.title: Denial of goods, services, facility or accommodation
provisions.content: A person shall not deny access to goods, services, facility or accommodation to a PLHIV or to a person perceived to be HIV positive on the ground of his HIV status.

provisions.title: Acts of discrimination
provisions.content: (1) A person shall be considered to discriminate a person living with HIV because of his HIV status if that person— (a) refuses to employ or continue to employ any person with HIV; (b) publishes any advertisement for employment or making any written inquiry which expresses or implies any limitation, specification or preference based on HIV status; (c) excludes a person from membership of any organisation because of his HIV status; (d) expels or suspends a member of any organisation for the reason that he is HIV positive; and (e) refuses to promote any person on the ground of HIV status. (2) The Ministry shall develop information, education and communication materials to address stigma and discrimination against people living with HIV.

provisions.title: Medical or health practitioner to provide health care service
provisions.content: Pursuant to section 29 of the Act , a medical or health practitioner shall not refuse to provide health care service to a PLHIV regardless of his physical appearance or behavior.

provisions.title: Duty of religious leader
provisions.content: A religious leader shall, during religious service, avoid the use of words which may encourage stigma and discrimination to persons living with HIV, orphans or their families.

provisions.title: Duty of public leader
provisions.content: Any public leader shall not use any words or conduct which discriminate PLHIV.

provisions.title: Duty of community
provisions.content: The community shall have a duty to support PLHIV and provide services without discrimination . Part XIV – Miscellaneous provisions

provisions.title: Resources for HIV and AIDS programmes
provisions.content: The resources allocated to programmes on HIV and AIDS shall be used for their intended purpose and equitably distributed.

provisions.title: Offence and penalty
provisions.content: Any person who contravenes the provisions of these Regulations commits an offence and shall, upon conviction, be liable to a penalty as prescribed in the Act .

provisions.title: Revocation of GN NO. 42 of 2011
provisions.content: The HIV and AIDS (Counselling and Testing, Use of ARVs and Disclosure) Regulations, 2010 are hereby revoked. First Schedule (Made under regulation 5(1)) Client’s consent form for HIV testing [Please note: Form has not been reproduced. Please refer to the publication document.] Second Schedule (Made under regulation 6(3)) Parents/guardian's consent form for testing children under 15 years and persons with communication disabilities [Please note: Form has not been reproduced. Please refer to the publication document.] Third Schedule (Made under regulation 21) Laboratory investigation request form [Please note: Forms have not been reproduced. Please refer to the publication document.] Fifth Schedule (Made under regulation 23(2)(b)(ii)) Client's referral form [Please note: Forms have not been reproduced. Please refer to the publication document.] Sixth Schedule (Made under regulation 31(3)) Report of suspected adverse reaction to medicines or vaccines [Please note: Forms have not been reproduced. Please refer to the publication document.] History of this document 25 February 2022 amendment not yet applied Amended by HIV and AIDS (Prevention and Control) (Amendment) Regulations, 2022 18 September 2020 this version Published in Gazette of the United Republic of Tanzania 38 Commenced 28 August 2020 Assented to Related documents Revokes HIV and AIDS (Counselling and Testing, Use of ARVs and Disclosure) Regulations, 2010 Is amended by HIV and AIDS (Prevention and Control) (Amendment) Regulations, 2022 More documents like this one [] [] [] [] ▲ To the top > Contact us Visit our Twitter/X profile Visit our Linked In profile Visit our YouTube channel About TanzLII TanzLII is operated by the Judiciary of Tanzania and the Office of the Attorney General, Chief Parliamentary Draftsman, and publishes the law of Tanzania for free online access for all. blood and other body fluids from human body that may be infectious and include semen, vaginal secretions and amniotic fluid

a distinction, exclusion or restriction, whether direct or indirect, made on the basis of the actual or perceived HIV status of a person living with or affected by HIV which has the effect or purpose of impairing or nullifying the recognition, enjoyment or exercise by that person on the basis of equality with other members of the community, on the human rights and fundamental freedoms in the political, economic, social, cultural, civil or any other field

the discharge, deposit, injection, dumping, spilling, leaking, abandoning or placing of medical waste in, on, into or onto land, other surface, building, vehicle, trailer or other containment structure or into any water, watercourse, storm water system or sewer system
